column5: Key Events
column6: Political/Religious Impact

column5: Neolithic Revolution (Domestication of crops/animals in Fertile Crescent)
column6:
- Transition from hunter-gatherers to settled agriculture
- Early religious shrines (e.g., Göbekli Tepe)

column5: Chalcolithic Period (Copper Age)
column6:
- First metallurgy; complex societies emerge
- Canaanite cultic practices (bull imagery, goddess worship)
column12: - Temple at Ein Gedi (4th millennium BCE)

column5: Bronze Age (Rise of City-States, Egypt, Mesopotamia)
column6:
- Canaan as crossroads of empires (Egypt, Hittites, Assyria)
- Amorite migrations (possible link to Hebrews)

column5: Middle Bronze Age (Patriarchal Era; Hyksos in Egypt)
column6:
- Abrahamic covenant; seminomadic pastoralism

column5: Age of Patriarchs begins (Abraham migrates from Ur to Canaan)
column6:
- Covenant with Abraham establishes foundational Jewish identity
- Early monotheistic beliefs contrast with Mesopotamian polytheism
column7: Genesis 12–25, Mesopotamian migration records (Ur III period)1, Ebla tablets (Syria)
column12:
- Abraham’s journey reflects Amorite migrations
- Covenant includes land and descendants promised

column5: Hebrews in Canaan & Egypt (Isaac, Jacob, Joseph’s rise in Egypt)
column6:
- Tribal confederation forms
- Famine leads to migration to Egypt (Hyksos period possible overlap)
column7: Genesis 37–50, Egyptian Execration Texts (mention of Canaanite tribes)
column12: - Joseph’s vizier role under Hyksos or early New Kingdom debated

column5: Late Bronze Age (Egyptian Domination, Exodus Debate)
column6:
- New Kingdom Egypt controls Canaan
- Possible Exodus during Ramesside period (13th cent. BCE)
column7: Merneptah Stele ("Israel" mentioned, 1208 BCE), Papyrus Anastasi I

column5: Exodus from Egypt under Moses
column6:
- Monotheism solidified (Yahweh as national deity)
- Torah received at Sinai (foundation of Israelite law)
column7: Book of Exodus, Egyptian records (Merneptah Stele, 1208 BCE)13
column12:
- 40 years of wilderness wandering
- Possible link to Habiru rebels in Amarna letters

column5: Late Bronze Collapse & Israelite Settlement (Iron Age I)
column6:
- Sea Peoples disrupt empires; Philistines settle coast
- Israelites emerge in highlands
column7: Book of Judges, Merneptah Stele, Philistine pottery

column5: Israelite Settlement in Canaan (Judges Period)
column6:
- Tribal conflicts with Philistines, Canaanites

column7: Book of Judges, Merneptah Stele ("Israel" as a people), archaeological evidence (highland settlements)
column12: - Possible connection to Sea Peoples’ disruptions

column5: Iron Age II (United Monarchy to Babylonian Exile)
column6:
- Davidic dynasty; centralized Temple worship
- Prophetic critique of kings
column7: Tel Dan Stele ("House of David"), Mesha Stele (Moab)
column12: - Split into Israel (N) and Judah (S) post-930 BCE

column5: First Temple Period (United Monarchy to Babylonian Exile)
column6:
- Centralized worship in Jerusalem
- Prophetic tradition emerges (critique of kings)
column7: Hebrew Bible (Kings, Chronicles), Mesha Stele (Moabite perspective), Babylonian Chronicles12
column12: - Temple destroyed by Nebuchadnezzar II (586 BCE)

column5: Fall of Northern Kingdom (Israel) to Assyria
column6:
- Ten Lost Tribes dispersed
- Samaritans emerge (mixed worship)
- Refugees flee to Judah (strengthening Jerusalem)

column5: Isaiah of Jerusalem – Prophesied Assyrian threat
column6:
- Influenced messianic expectations
- Later reinterpreted by Christians
column7: Book of Isaiah, Dead Sea Scrolls (1QIsaa)

column5: Babylonian Exile (Judeans deported to Babylon)
column6:
- Synagogue worship develops
- Monotheism reinforced (Ezekiel’s visions)

column5: Jeremiah – Warned of Babylonian exile
column6:

column5: Babylonian Destruction of Jerusalem
column6:
- Exile to Babylon; monotheism refined
- Synagogue worship begins

column5: Achaemenid Persian Rule
column6:
- Second Temple built (515 BCE)

column5: Ezekiel – Visions in exile
column6:
- Shaped apocalyptic Judaism

column5: Ezra-Nehemiah reforms (Torah codification)

column5: Socrates – Athenian philosopher

column5: Aristotle – Tutored Alexander
column6:
